title: C# Advanced — Paper 1
description: Advanced C# covering Generics, Generic Variance, LINQ Core & Advanced Operators, Lambda & Functional Patterns, Advanced Exception Handling, Memory Management, Span<T>/Memory<T>, Reflection, and Attributes.

questions.text: Which constraint syntax ensures a generic type parameter T is a reference type and has a public parameterless constructor?

options.text: where T : struct, new()
where T : class, new()
where T : class
where T : new(), object

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of the 'out' keyword on a generic type parameter in C#?

options.text: It allows the type to be used only as a method output parameter.
It marks the parameter as contravariant so it can appear only in input positions.
It marks the parameter as covariant so it can appear only in output positions, enabling assignment of IFoo<Derived> to IFoo<Base>.
It allows boxing of value types within the generic type.

questions.text: What does the LINQ operator GroupBy return when applied to a sequence?

options.text: IEnumerable<TElement> with elements sorted by key
IEnumerable<IGrouping<TKey, TElement>> where each IGrouping represents elements sharing the same key
ILookup<TKey, TElement> directly
Dictionary<TKey, List<TElement>>

questions.text: Which LINQ method flattens a sequence of sequences into a single sequence?

options.text: SelectMany
Flatten
Merge
Concat

questions.text: What is a closure in C# lambda expressions?

options.text: A lambda that cannot capture any variables from its enclosing scope
A lambda that captures and retains references to variables from its enclosing scope, even after the scope exits
A sealed class that wraps a delegate
A lambda expression that is immediately invoked upon declaration

questions.text: What exception type wraps multiple exceptions thrown during parallel or async operations in .NET?

options.text: MultiException
CompositeException
AggregateException
ParallelException

questions.text: Which interface should a class implement to properly release unmanaged resources in C#?

options.text: IReleasable
IDisposable
ICleanup
IFinalizable

questions.text: What is a key characteristic of Span<T> that distinguishes it from arrays?

options.text: Span<T> can be stored on the heap as a class field.
Span<T> is a ref struct restricted to stack allocation, providing safe slice views of contiguous memory without additional heap allocation.
Span<T> automatically resizes when elements are added.
Span<T> supports asynchronous enumeration natively.

questions.text: Which class in the System.Reflection namespace is used to dynamically create an instance of a type given its Type object?

options.text: TypeBuilder
Activator
RuntimeHelper
InstanceFactory

questions.text: Which built-in attribute instructs the compiler to mark a method as obsolete and generate a warning when it is called?

options.text: [Deprecated]
[Removed]
[Obsolete]
[Legacy]

questions.text: In C# generics, which constraint is used to restrict a type parameter to value types only?

options.text: where T : value
where T : struct
where T : ValueType
where T : unmanaged

questions.text: Why can IEnumerable<string> be assigned to IEnumerable<object> in C#, but List<string> cannot be assigned to List<object>?

options.text: List<T> is sealed, preventing such assignments.
IEnumerable<T> is covariant (out T), while List<T> is invariant because it supports both reading and writing.
IEnumerable<T> implements an implicit cast operator.
List<T> uses contravariance (in T) which blocks the assignment.

questions.text: Which LINQ method syntax is equivalent to 'from x in source where x > 5 select x'?

options.text: source.Filter(x => x > 5)
source.Where(x => x > 5).Select(x => x)
source.Select(x => x > 5)
source.Query(x => x > 5)

questions.text: What is deferred execution in LINQ?

options.text: LINQ queries execute immediately when defined and cache results.
LINQ queries define a pipeline that executes only when the result is enumerated, not when the query is declared.
LINQ queries run in a background thread automatically.
LINQ queries are compiled at runtime for deferred JIT optimization.

questions.text: In the context of closures, what happens when a loop variable is captured by a lambda?

options.text: Each iteration's lambda captures its own independent copy of the loop variable.
All lambdas share the same reference to the loop variable, so they all see its final value after the loop ends.
The compiler automatically copies the loop variable into each lambda.
Capturing a loop variable is a compile-time error in C#.

questions.text: What does ExceptionDispatchInfo.Capture() allow you to do in C#?

options.text: It logs exception details to the event log automatically.
It captures an exception including its stack trace and allows rethrowing it later while preserving the original stack trace.
It converts checked exceptions to unchecked exceptions.
It prevents exceptions from propagating across thread boundaries.

questions.text: In the Dispose pattern, when should GC.SuppressFinalize(this) be called?

options.text: In the finalizer to prevent double disposal
In the Dispose() method to tell the GC not to call the finalizer since resources are already cleaned up
In the constructor to skip finalization entirely
Only when the object holds managed resources

questions.text: What does stackalloc do in C#?

options.text: It allocates an array on the managed heap with pinning.
It allocates a block of memory on the stack, which is automatically freed when the method returns, usable with Span<T>.
It increases the thread stack size dynamically at runtime.
It allocates memory in the large object heap.

questions.text: How do you retrieve all public instance methods of a type using reflection?

options.text: typeof(MyType).GetMethods(BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance)
typeof(MyType).GetPublicMethods()
MyType.Methods.Where(m => m.IsPublic)
Assembly.GetMethods(typeof(MyType))

questions.text: What does the AttributeUsage attribute control when applied to a custom attribute class?

options.text: It controls the access modifier of the attribute class.
It specifies where the attribute can be applied (e.g., classes, methods), whether it can appear multiple times, and whether it is inherited.
It determines the serialization format of the attribute.
It marks which assembly the attribute belongs to.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly declares a generic method that returns the larger of two IComparable<T> values?

options.text: T Max<T>(T a, T b) where T : IComparable<T> => a.CompareTo(b) >= 0 ? a : b;
T Max(T a, T b) => a > b ? a : b;
T Max<T>(T a, T b) => a.CompareTo(b);
object Max<T>(T a, T b) where T : struct => a;

questions.text: Which of the following demonstrates contravariance in C# generics?

options.text: IEnumerable<string> assigned to IEnumerable<object>
Action<object> assigned to Action<string> because Action<T> is contravariant (in T)
Func<string> assigned to Func<object>
List<int> assigned to List<object>

questions.text: What does the LINQ Aggregate method do?

options.text: It groups elements by a key and returns IGrouping<TKey,T>.
It applies an accumulator function over a sequence, reducing it to a single result.
It merges two sequences by index.
It counts distinct elements in a sequence.

questions.text: In a lambda expression, what does capturing a variable 'by reference' (via closure) mean in practice?

options.text: The lambda creates a value copy of the variable at the time of creation.
The lambda holds a reference to the variable's storage location, so changes to it inside or outside the lambda are visible to both.
The variable is passed using the ref keyword explicitly.
The lambda pins the variable in memory to prevent GC collection.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'checked' keyword in C# exception handling contexts?

options.text: It marks a code block where null reference exceptions are automatically caught.
It enables overflow checking for arithmetic operations, causing an OverflowException to be thrown on integer overflow instead of silent wrapping.
It verifies that all variables are initialized before use.
It checks that all interface members are implemented at runtime.

questions.text: What is the difference between a finalizer and Dispose() in C#?

options.text: Finalizers are called deterministically; Dispose() is called by the GC.
Dispose() is called deterministically by developer code or 'using'; finalizers are called non-deterministically by the GC to clean up unmanaged resources if Dispose was not called.
Both finalizers and Dispose() are called at the same time by the GC.
Finalizers can only clean up managed resources; Dispose() handles unmanaged resources.

questions.text: What is the difference between Span<T> and Memory<T> in C#?

options.text: Memory<T> is stack-only while Span<T> can be stored on the heap.
Span<T> is a ref struct restricted to the stack; Memory<T> is a regular struct that can be stored on the heap and used with async methods.
They are interchangeable and have identical restrictions.
Memory<T> only works with unmanaged types; Span<T> works with all types.

questions.text: How can you use reflection to invoke a private method named 'Calculate' on an object instance?

options.text: obj.Calculate() directly, since reflection bypasses access modifiers automatically.
var method = obj.GetType().GetMethod("Calculate", BindingFlags.NonPublic | BindingFlags.Instance); method.Invoke(obj, null);
Activator.InvokePrivate(obj, "Calculate");
dynamic d = obj; d.Calculate();

questions.text: How do you read a custom attribute of type MyAttr from a class MyClass at runtime using reflection?

options.text: MyClass.GetAttribute<MyAttr>()
typeof(MyClass).GetCustomAttribute<MyAttr>()
Attribute.Find(typeof(MyClass), typeof(MyAttr))
MyClass.Attributes.OfType<MyAttr>().First()

questions.text: Which of the following correctly defines a generic class Stack<T> that constrains T to be a reference type?

options.text: class Stack<T> where T : object { }
class Stack<T> where T : class { }
class Stack<T> where T : reference { }
class Stack<T> where T : IClass { }
